note: Each tariff is URDB-derived and engine-validated (fully priceable by the ratebook engine). Confidence high/medium/low; verify against your bill — see per-entry notes.

tariffs.label: 69deb3d8e547dde41109ee02
tariffs.note: Canonical AL default residential ('Family Dwelling Service' is Alabama Power's standard whole-house residential rate). Seasonal: 2 periods, tiered. First-tier rate $0.1447181/kWh, $16/mo base charge. Effective 2026-01-01. fully_supported=True. No clean engine-supported residential TOU (PAE/demand options excluded).

tariffs.label: 67744be6b5f9d561220eced9
tariffs.note: Ameren Missouri (legal name Union Electric Co, eiaid 19436) standard default residential rate: the 1(M) Anytime (flat, non-TOU) rate. Distinct from Ameren Illinois (different utility). Current 2024-09-30 start, no enddate. 2 periods (summer/winter) tiered.

tariffs.label: 677451bcb5f9d561220eceda
tariffs.note: Ameren Missouri default TOU option: 1(M) Smart Saver Service. Current 2024-09-30 start, no enddate. 6 TOU periods; first (on-peak) tier 0.413. Several other TOU 'saver' variants exist (Evening/Morning, Overnight, Ultimate, Anytime-with-peak); Smart Saver is the standard residential TOU offering.

tariffs.label: 69a706ab7da20ea6b00d0238
tariffs.note: APS's current default residential service plan (new customers default to R-TOU-E). Validates fully_supported; first-period (off-peak winter) effective rate 0.153281 $/kWh; summer on-peak ~0.37. Fixed charge is $0.458/day = ~$13.94/mo. Chosen as the APS pick because the nominal standard plan (R-1 Fixed Energy Charge) is broken in URDB: it is split into separate 'Small Tier 1 / Medium Tier 2 / Large Tier 3' records and the Tier-1 fragment carries an implausible flat 0.42756 $/kWh, while 'Saver Choice Plus' (R-Basic) is unsupported due to a demand charge. Current (start 2026-02-01, no enddate), eiaid 803.

tariffs.label: 69a72480d189752a6d0863a9
tariffs.note: Canonical residential default (Schedule R), effective 2026-01-01, no enddate. Rate ~0.0624/kWh is delivery-only (MD deregulated; supply via SOS/competitive). Newest of several duplicate rows picked; supersedes the stale 2017 bundled row (0.1326).

tariffs.plan_name: Residential Delivery and Energy Time of Use (Schedule RD)
tariffs.label: 69aee828043957d3e90229a8
tariffs.note: Default residential TOU companion; Schedule RD shows genuine time differentiation (off-peak 0.04368 / peak 0.12931). Effective 2026-03-01. Chosen over Schedule RL (Optional TOU) whose rows were flat across periods (no usable peak/off-peak spread).

tariffs.plan_name: BES - Residential Single Family Without Electric Space Heat Delivery Class
tariffs.label: 679c05efaddfd4b96500e1d4
tariffs.note: Illinois is deregulated; ComEd is a delivery-only utility. BES = Basic Electric Service, the default-supply rate for non-shopping single-family customers (no electric space heat). All-in rate ~0.129/kWh is plausible. Current (2025-01-01, no enddate). Engine reports 2 periods (summer/non-summer) single tier. This is the closest thing to a canonical default whole-house ComEd rate. RDS/BESH variants are unbundled delivery-only and were avoided.

tariffs.plan_name: SC-1 - Residential & Religious Service - Zone J [NYC]
tariffs.label: 67b4e33e7abe0c080a0b1171
tariffs.note: Canonical whole-house residential default (SC-1) for ConEd's primary NYC Zone J territory; full bundled rate (delivery+supply), 2-tier (>250 kWh steps to 0.39251). Effective 2025-02-01, no enddate. ~0.37/kWh is accurate for ConEd NYC, among highest in US.

tariffs.plan_name: SC-1 Residential & Religious Voluntary TOD (Rate III) - Zone J [NYC]
tariffs.label: 67b4ee9d4beb07f0d40cb9eb
tariffs.note: Default residential TOU companion for NYC Zone J; full bundled 3-period TOU (peak ~0.492). Effective 2025-02-01, no enddate. Chose Rate III over the Rate II variant whose 0.0449 figure reflected delivery-only components.

tariffs.plan_name: Residential Service Secondary Non-Transmitting Meter Rate (RSM)
tariffs.label: 6793f1d7055fb118920d2308
tariffs.note: Consumers Energy standard whole-house residential rate (RSM). Current 2025-01-01, no enddate. 2 periods (summer tiered 0.228/0.282 + winter 0.228). Duplicate label 6793f1de055fb118920d2309 captures only a single 0.141 partial tier; selected the complete tiered capture. Smart Hours (RSH) and Nighttime Savers (RPM) are optional variants and were avoided.

tariffs.label: 6793d5f0408c15764c05042b
tariffs.note: Consumers Energy default TOU rate: RSP (Residential Summer On-Peak Basic Service), the default-enrolled summer peak-pricing rate. Current 2025-01-01, no enddate. 3 periods (0.233 / 0.282 / 0.228). Duplicate label 6793d5e9408c15764c05042a captures only a single 0.141 partial off-peak tier; selected the complete multi-period capture. tariff_type marked seasonal/TOU.

tariffs.label: 6997c46543b6aa9b2a015f78
tariffs.note: Canonical default whole-house residential service for Dominion (Virginia Electric & Power Co). Current effective 2026-01-01, no enddate. Two periods = summer/winter seasonal. eiaid 19876. Engine fully_supported, bundled rate plausible.

tariffs.label: 6997c7bed3c1f5b8140d5aab
tariffs.note: Default residential energy time-of-use option for Dominion VA. Current effective 2026-01-01, no enddate. Chosen over Schedule 1S (demand TOU, engine rejects: demandratestructure present).

tariffs.plan_name: Residential Time of Day (Full Service) - D1.2
tariffs.label: 69e685c97e0cd194c00ddacb
tariffs.note: DTE default TOU option: D1.2 Time of Day Full Service. Current 2026-04-01, no enddate. 4 TOU periods (0.289 on-peak summer / 0.182 / 0.264 / 0.180). Duplicate label 69e685c4b710e71bf20702bc captures only a single 0.107 partial tier; selected the complete 4-period capture.

tariffs.label: 69e681d00b9e1bb34f0912d9
tariffs.note: Canonical DTE standard residential default: D1 Full Service (bundled supply+delivery, the default for non-shopping customers). Current 2026-04-01, no enddate. Single period with 2 tiers (0.215 / 0.228). NOTE: a duplicate label 69e681c4578eb5797e0856bb exists for the same plan/source but captures only a single partial 0.106 tier (likely delivery-only fragment); I selected the complete bundled capture.

tariffs.label: 6998c205a746c90e550cb23f
tariffs.note: Canonical NC default residential. Single flat energy rate $0.1276/kWh, $14/mo basic facilities charge. Effective 2026-01-01, no enddate. fully_supported=True.

tariffs.label: 69e26f920ccfe8b33d05b67c
tariffs.note: Duke Energy Florida appears in URDB under legacy name 'Progress Energy Florida Inc' (eiaid 6455). Canonical default residential RS-1, seasonal (2 periods: summer/winter) each with 2 usage tiers. First-tier winter rate $0.13502/kWh. Effective 2026-02-01. fully_supported=True.

tariffs.plan_name: RST-1 Residential Service Time of Use
tariffs.label: 678abac33d12e18b730b0663
tariffs.note: Default TOU option for Duke FL (Progress Energy Florida Inc). 3 energy periods (seasonal TOU). First-period rate $0.17289/kWh. Effective 2025-01-01. fully_supported=True.

tariffs.label: 699c81cba137818a0d03e869
tariffs.note: El Paso Electric standard default residential service (Schedule 01, Texas). Seasonal tiered (summer 0.129512/0.134695, winter 0.119146 $/kWh). Validates fully_supported. Current (start 2025-01-01, no enddate), eiaid 5701. EPE serves west Texas and southern New Mexico; this is the Texas jurisdiction record.

tariffs.label: 69a99f187cef8411ae01894b
tariffs.note: Entergy Louisiana Inc. RS is the standard residential default. Current effective 2026-03-02 (most recent Entergy filing in corpus), no enddate. eiaid 11241. Flat bundled rate ~$0.081/kWh, consistent with low Louisiana pricing. Chosen as the primary Entergy pick.

tariffs.label: 67d32c3fe3755f399109c6fd
tariffs.note: Entergy Texas Inc. standard residential service (Entergy TX is a vertically-integrated non-ERCOT utility, so it has a genuine bundled default rate unlike the deregulated ERCOT TDUs). Effective 2025-01-02, no enddate. eiaid 55937. Two seasonal periods.

tariffs.label: 67d32c46e3755f399109c6fe
tariffs.note: Entergy Texas Inc. default residential time-of-day option. Effective 2025-01-02, no enddate. Three TOU periods, bundled. Companion TOU plan for Entergy TX.

tariffs.label: 6969332bd41988757a0409fc
tariffs.note: Connecticut Light & Power Co d/b/a Eversource. Rate 1 is the standard residential default. Current effective 2026-01-01, no enddate. eiaid 4176. Bundled flat rate. Chosen as the flagship Eversource pick because it is fully bundled (the MA NSTAR R-1 entry is delivery-only).

tariffs.label: 6969389492370159da017a0d
tariffs.note: Connecticut Light & Power Co / Eversource default residential time-of-day. Current effective 2026-01-01, no enddate. Two TOU periods, bundled.

tariffs.label: 69a9c4fc2f2357589100972d
tariffs.note: Canonical FPL default residential. Single period, 2 usage tiers ($0.12258 first tier / $0.14258 above 1000 kWh). $10.52/mo customer charge. Effective 2026-01-01. fully_supported=True.

tariffs.plan_name: RTR-1 Residential Service Time of Use
tariffs.label: 69a9c7c368741bcb070c9cec
tariffs.note: FPL default residential TOU. Genuine TOU: off-peak ~$0.12258 vs on-peak $0.26894/kWh (verified distinct periods). energy_rate is the first-period/off-peak base tier. Effective 2026-01-01. fully_supported=True.

tariffs.label: 677c384de1bf88853903410c
tariffs.note: Canonical GA default residential. Seasonal: 2 periods (summer single-rate $0.135222, winter 3 tiers). Basic charge is $0.4603/DAY (~$14.00/mo, converted). Effective 2025-01-01. fully_supported=True.

tariffs.plan_name: Schedule TOU-OA-14 Time of Use - Overnight Advantage
tariffs.label: 677c39c3ef5537337508436e
tariffs.note: GA default residential TOU (Overnight Advantage), energy-only (no demand charge), so engine-supported. 3 periods; first-period/off-peak overnight rate $0.067439/kWh. Basic charge $0.4603/day (~$14/mo). Effective 2025-01-01. fully_supported=True. Chose this over TOU-RD-11 (has demand charge, unsupported) and TOU-REO-18.

tariffs.label: 6850a2359670d5240a0049af
tariffs.note: Idaho Power Co. Schedule 1 is the standard residential default. Current effective 2025-02-01, no enddate. eiaid 9191. Structure is seasonal (summer/winter) with inclining-block tiers (800/2000 kWh breakpoints); first-tier summer effective rate reported. Bundled.

tariffs.label: 6850a401cb14479d0d08ed7c
tariffs.note: Idaho Power Co default residential time-of-day. Current effective 2025-02-01, no enddate. Five periods (seasonal + TOU). Companion TOU plan for Idaho Power.

tariffs.utility_display: Los Angeles Department of Water & Power (LADWP)
tariffs.label: 69eb933dd52ff0b5930ddce2
tariffs.note: LADWP canonical default residential tiered rate (R-1A). Zone 1 is the lower-temperature/standard zone (vs Zone 2 high-desert). No standalone fixed charge in this schedule. Effective 2026-04-01, current.

tariffs.utility_display: Los Angeles Department of Water & Power (LADWP)
tariffs.label: 69eb931cf253a30c3f0075f1
tariffs.note: LADWP default residential TOU rate (R-1B). 0.221986 is the first-period effective rate. Includes ~$8/mo service charge. Effective 2026-04-01, current.

tariffs.label: 67cf32f6ef8f02942e0586bd
tariffs.note: Massachusetts Electric Co d/b/a National Grid. R-1 is the standard residential default. Current effective 2025-03-01, no enddate. eiaid 11804. Rate is bundled (delivery + basic service), ~$0.287/kWh, consistent with MA all-in pricing.

tariffs.label: 5cd5ae985457a30a0454e9d2
tariffs.note: Massachusetts Electric Co optional residential TOU. No enddate (open). Source dates to 2019 rate filing; older than the R-1 pick, so supply component may be stale - flagged medium. Two TOU periods.

tariffs.label: 67bcc01d2d5615df800a181d
tariffs.note: Niagara Mohawk Power Corp d/b/a National Grid. SC1 is the standard residential service; rates are zone-based (A-F) - Zone A picked as the canonical reference zone. Current effective 2025-01-30, no enddate. eiaid 13573. Bundled (delivery+supply) flat rate.

tariffs.label: 67bcd40e7aa26ac17e0f327d
tariffs.note: Niagara Mohawk / National Grid optional residential TOU (SC1C). Zone A reference zone. Effective 2023-02-01, no enddate. 3 TOU periods. Fixed charge is high because it bundles delivery basic-service plus supply components.

tariffs.plan_name: ORS-TOU (Optional Residential Service Time of Use)
tariffs.label: 69ea606ff253a30c3f0075ee
tariffs.note: Nevada Power's optional residential time-of-use plan (ORS-TOU). Validates fully_supported; first-period (off-peak) effective rate 0.09171 $/kWh. Current (start 2026-04-01, no enddate), eiaid 13407.

tariffs.label: 69ea5f990b9e1bb34f0912de
tariffs.note: Nevada Power's standard default residential service (RS), single flat energy rate 0.12135 $/kWh. Validates fully_supported. Current (start 2026-04-01, no enddate), eiaid 13407. Nevada Power is the NV Energy southern (Las Vegas) operating company.

tariffs.plan_name: Schedule 1 - Residential Service (Single Phase, single family)
tariffs.label: 685c51a57ab730a74905690c
tariffs.note: PacifiCorp's standard residential default, taken from its largest jurisdiction (Utah / Rocky Mountain Power). Schedule 1, single-phase single-family. Seasonal tiered (summer 0.114972 first tier; winter lower). Validates fully_supported. Current (start 2025-06-01, no enddate), eiaid 14354. PacifiCorp does business as Rocky Mountain Power (UT/WY/ID) and Pacific Power (OR/WA/CA); Utah chosen as the representative default.

tariffs.label: 69c30f2893a800cd5603da5c
tariffs.note: PacifiCorp Utah optional residential time-of-use (Schedule 1 TOU). Validates fully_supported; first-period effective rate 0.089833 $/kWh. Current (start 2026-01-01, no enddate), eiaid 14354. Confidence medium only because the URDB plan name does not carry the official Utah TOU schedule number; rate magnitude and structure are consistent with a residential TOU.

tariffs.label: 69e650226746522c70079989
tariffs.note: Canonical residential default (Rate R), single flat period, effective 2026-01-01, no enddate. ~0.116/kWh is plausible (PA deregulated but this row appears to include default-service supply). One of two identical duplicate rows; picked one.

tariffs.label: 69e65a18805b2131910774d9
tariffs.note: Default residential TOU companion; 3-period with strong differentiation (peak 0.43607 / off-peak 0.16921). Effective 2026-01-01, no enddate.

tariffs.label: 68b397ff25d85a1c070f0a0d
tariffs.note: Canonical residential default (Schedule R) for Pepco's DC territory (eia 15270). Rate ~0.034/kWh first tier is delivery-only (DC deregulated; supply via SOS/competitive). Tiered (summer step to 0.06591). Effective 2025-08-01, no enddate. Pepco also has a Maryland affiliate but its only current residential URDB row is a closed EV plan, so DC is used.

tariffs.label: 59653cee5457a395794c9f92
tariffs.note: Only available residential TOU (Schedule RTM); 6-period. Carries enddate=None so flagged current in URDB, but startdate is 2017-06-01 and rate basis (~0.13 bundled) differs from the 2025 delivery-only Schedule R, so it is a stale vintage. Include with caution or skip if a fresh RTM is preferred.

tariffs.label: 69d92c23ac3477692606714c
tariffs.note: PG&E canonical default residential tiered rate (E-1). Region P (San Francisco / coastal) chosen as the representative baseline region; PG&E E-1 is split into 10 climate baseline regions (P-Z), all identical structure differing only by baseline allowance. Effective 2026-03-27, current (no enddate). Fixed charge is the ~$0.79/mo minimum bill component.

tariffs.plan_name: E-TOU-C Residential Time of Use (Baseline Region P)
tariffs.label: 69de6fa62aa9e9994f0ed2bd
tariffs.note: PG&E default residential TOU rate (E-TOU-C), Region P. Peak/off-peak with baseline credit. 0.441 is the first (peak) period tier-1 effective rate; off-peak periods are lower. Effective 2026-03-27, current.

tariffs.label: 69794d4e3832714bb307e13d
tariffs.note: Portland General Electric canonical default residential rate (Schedule 7), seasonal. Effective 2026-01-01, current. Three identical 2026-01-01 duplicate rows exist in URDB (69794d4e..., 69794de1..., 69794f3f...); 69794de1 fails to load (malformed open-max tier), so chose 69794d4e which validates with the full bundled-rate figure (0.1831/kWh incl. all riders). Fixed basic charge ~$16.36/mo.

tariffs.label: 6979502b3b4e00e41e0d686a
tariffs.note: Portland General Electric default residential TOU rate (Schedule 7 TOU). 0.1106 is the first (off-peak) period effective rate. Effective 2026-01-01, current. URDB name labels it 'Rate 7-TOU or EV' - it is the residential TOU option under Schedule 7 (not an EV-only rate). Same $16.36/mo basic charge.

tariffs.label: 69aefb5ecdc3e6e8f607286c
tariffs.note: Canonical residential default (RS), single flat period, effective 2025-12-01, no enddate. Rate ~0.050/kWh is delivery-only (PA deregulated; PTC/default supply procured separately). Legitimate current PPL distribution tariff. One of two identical duplicate rows; picked one. No current residential TOU exists in URDB for PPL.

tariffs.label: 69e244d3a999a8521d0fed8c
tariffs.note: Canonical residential default (RS). Rate ~0.060/kWh is delivery-only because NJ is deregulated and supply (BGS) is procured separately/competitively; this is the legitimate current PSE&G utility tariff effective 2026-04-01. One of two identical duplicate rows; picked one.

tariffs.label: 69e24a7a4db539998e061b9c
tariffs.note: Default residential TOU companion; 6-period schedule with genuine time differentiation (peak ~0.225, super-off-peak ~0.033). Effective 2026-04-01, no enddate.

tariffs.label: 66cc9e270d535cf5c3058518
tariffs.note: SCE canonical default residential tiered rate (Schedule D / Domestic Service). Region 5 chosen as representative; SCE splits Schedule D into climate baseline regions (5,6,8,9,10,13,14,15,16) with identical structure. Fixed charge is the small daily minimum-charge component (~$0.031/day). Effective 2024-06-01, current.

tariffs.label: 66ccb7bfa7d4ef64fc07ad6b
tariffs.note: SCE default residential TOU rate (TOU-D-4-9PM, 4-9pm peak window). 0.37846 is the first-period effective rate; peak periods reach ~0.60. Two identical-source duplicate rows exist in URDB (66ccb7bfa7d4ef64fc07ad6b and 66ccb7ae0a0eb5212a062238); chose the one with full seasonal peak/off-peak differentiation. Effective 2024-06-01, current.

tariffs.plan_name: Schedule DR Residential Service (Coastal Baseline Region)
tariffs.label: 6772eaee739c802b3b0a6f3d
tariffs.note: SDG&E canonical standard residential tiered rate (Schedule DR). Coastal baseline region chosen as representative (also Desert/Inland/Mountain). No fixed monthly charge - SDG&E uses a minimum bill. Effective 2024-09-30, current. URDB source was a transient blob: URL; substituted SDG&E pricing-plans page as stable source.

tariffs.plan_name: Schedule TOU-DR-1 Residential Time of Use (Coastal Baseline Region)
tariffs.label: 6772cfc9b5f9d561220ececb
tariffs.note: SDG&E default residential TOU rate (TOU-DR-1) - the plan most SDG&E residential customers default to. 0.59976 is the on-peak (first) period effective rate; off-peak periods drop to ~0.25, consistent with SDG&E's high CA rates. Coastal region. No fixed charge (minimum bill). Effective 2024-09-30, current. URDB source was a transient blob: URL; substituted SDG&E pricing page.

tariffs.label: 62c852e709e7863b6f399106
tariffs.note: SMUD canonical standard residential rate (Schedule R), seasonal (summer/winter). Validates cleanly with plausible rate (0.117/kWh) and fixed System Infrastructure charge (~$23/mo). CAUTION: the corpus copy is stale - this 2022-03-01 version has enddate 2023-08-21 and SMUD has NOT been refreshed since; the only rows with open enddate are 'Closed'/pilot variants. Included because it is the genuine default plan, but rates predate 2024-2026 increases. Recommend refreshing from SMUD before launch.

tariffs.plan_name: E-23 Basic Price Plan for Residential Service (Tier 1)
tariffs.label: 69a1b8bf40140c3bb007f1bd
tariffs.note: SRP's standard default residential price plan (E-23 Basic). Seasonal tiered (winter 0.1204 / summer-peak 0.1398 / summer 0.1097 $/kWh). Validates fully_supported. URDB splits E-23 into Tier 1/2/3 records; Tier 1 chosen as the canonical first-tier representation. Current (start 2026-01-01, no enddate), eiaid 16572. SRP is a public power district not regulated by the ACC.

tariffs.label: 69a1d19551a2a4045a02f890
tariffs.note: SRP's standard residential time-of-use plan (E-26). Validates fully_supported; first-period effective rate 0.0891 $/kWh (off-peak), with on-peak higher. Current (start 2026-01-01, no enddate), eiaid 16572.

tariffs.label: 64c93817a0febfb2b5028d8a
tariffs.note: Canonical TECO default residential RS. Single period, 2 usage tiers ($0.13580 first / $0.15705 above 1000 kWh). Basic charge $0.71/day (~$21.60/mo, converted). fully_supported=True. CAVEAT: most recent RS in corpus is 2023 vintage (startdate 2023-01-01, enddate 2023-12-31) — no 2024+ Tampa rate is loaded, so rate is stale and should be refreshed before launch.

tariffs.label: 6859a625ee417fe3fa0cb0df
tariffs.note: We Energies (Wisconsin Electric Power Co, eiaid 20847) standard residential default: Rg 1 single-phase. Current 2025 rate book, no enddate. Engine validated as fully supported, flat rate ~0.141/kWh (plausible for WI). No fixed monthly charge captured (We Energies Rg1 has a per-day facilities charge structure rather than a flat monthly fixed charge in this capture).

tariffs.label: 6859a6a4417690873a01fd68
tariffs.note: We Energies default TOU option: Rg 2 Time-of-Use single-phase Option A (the first/standard of options A-D). Current 2025-06-01, no enddate. Engine reported fully_supported during validation. Listed as the canonical default TOU among the four Rg2 TOU options.

tariffs.utility_display: Xcel Energy - Northern States Power Minnesota
tariffs.label: 67a3b004f7f9d21dc80bbeaf
tariffs.note: Xcel Energy Minnesota (Northern States Power Co - Minnesota, eiaid 13781) standard residential default: A01 Overhead Standard (non-electric-space-heat). Current 2025-01-01, no enddate. 2 seasonal periods (0.174 / 0.156). Included as Xcel's other major operating company alongside PSCo Colorado.

tariffs.utility_display: Xcel Energy - Northern States Power Minnesota
tariffs.label: 67a3b148539f2d3f2c056974
tariffs.note: Xcel Minnesota default TOU option: A02 Time-of-Day Overhead Standard. Current 2025-01-01, no enddate. 4 TOU periods (0.088 off-peak / 0.258 / 0.088 / 0.315). Canonical TOD companion to A01.

tariffs.utility_display: Xcel Energy - Public Service Co of Colorado
tariffs.plan_name: Residential Energy Time Of Use (Schedule RE-TOU)
tariffs.label: 69bd927af5cd25efec0e9aad
tariffs.note: Xcel Colorado default TOU rate: Schedule RE-TOU (now the default residential rate in Colorado). Current 2026-01-01, no enddate. 4 TOU periods (0.328 on-peak / 0.122 / 0.381 / 0.139). Canonical TOU companion to Schedule R.

tariffs.utility_display: Xcel Energy - Public Service Co of Colorado
tariffs.label: 69bd99ef0bf1bbccd206e47b
tariffs.note: Xcel Energy Colorado (Public Service Co of Colorado, eiaid 15466) standard residential default: Schedule R. Current 2026-01-01, no enddate. 2 seasonal periods (0.156 / 0.173). Clean canonical name. PSCo is one of Xcel's two largest residential operating companies.
